pilot_overview: Four pilot experiments for Round 3 (Candidate C: Synthetic-to-Real Transfer). Pilots validate the core pipeline: synthetic data generation -> SAE training -> metric validation -> real SAE transfer. All pilots target 5-15 minute runtime on a single GPU.

pilot_decision_criteria.advance_to_full: SAE trains successfully (MSE < 5%) AND at least one metric achieves ROC-AUC > 0.75 on synthetic data AND real SAE transfer produces at least 5 identifiable parent-child pairs with computable metric scores
pilot_decision_criteria.refine: SAE trains but metric AUC 0.65-0.75 OR real SAE transfer identifies pairs but metrics are noisy OR synthetic data structure is correct but SAE reconstruction is marginal (5-10% MSE)
pilot_decision_criteria.pivot: SAE fails to train (MSE > 10%) OR no metric achieves AUC > 0.65 OR real SAE has zero identifiable hierarchical features OR synthetic ground-truth labels are all same class

pilots.hypothesis: Synthetic hierarchical features can be generated with clear ground-truth absorption labels
pilots.description: Generate simple synthetic data with 50 features (10 parent + 40 child), 2-level hierarchy, controlled co-occurrence, low noise. Validate that ground-truth labels contain both absorbed and non-absorbed parents.
pilots.pass_criteria: Activation matrix shape = (1000, 128); ground-truth has both absorbed (p_alone < 0.2*p_given) and non-absorbed parents; parent-child co-occurrence visible in activation statistics
pilots.failure_action: If ground-truth is degenerate: adjust p_parent_alone and p_parent_given_child ranges; if still fails, flag synthetic generation as at-risk

pilots.hypothesis: Small SAE can be trained on synthetic data with good reconstruction and reasonable sparsity
pilots.description: Train Standard ReLU SAE (d_model=128, d_sae=512) on simple synthetic data. Validate reconstruction quality and sparsity.
pilots.pass_criteria: Reconstruction MSE < 5% of input variance; L0 (avg active features) between 10 and 100; loss decreases monotonically; no training crashes
pilots.failure_action: If MSE too high: increase d_sae or decrease L1; if L0 too low/high: adjust L1 coefficient; if unstable: reduce lr or increase batch size

pilots.hypothesis: At least one absorption metric achieves good accuracy (AUC > 0.75) on synthetic ground-truth data
pilots.description: Apply 4 metrics to trained synthetic SAE and compare to ground-truth labels. Compute ROC-AUC, precision, recall for each metric.

metrics.name: chanin_ablation
metrics.description: Chanin et al. ablation-based absorption: 1 - act_parent(child_zeroed) / act_parent(child_active)

metrics.name: saebench_absorption
metrics.description: SAEBench absorption metric implementation (or closest replication)

metrics.name: encoder_decoder_cosine
metrics.description: Cosine similarity between encoder and decoder weights per latent

metrics.name: conditional_activation
metrics.description: P(parent active | child off) / P(parent active | child on)

pilots.pass_criteria: At least one metric achieves ROC-AUC > 0.75; at least 10% of parents are absorbed in ground truth; metrics produce score distributions with non-zero variance
pilots.failure_action: If best AUC < 0.75: try combined metric (logistic regression on multiple signals); if still weak, consider that absorption may not be detectable with these metrics and flag for discussion

pilots.hypothesis: Calibrated metrics from synthetic data can be applied to real SAEs to produce meaningful absorption estimates
pilots.description: Apply best metric from pilot_metric_validation to GPT-2-small SAE (layer 8). Identify parent-child pairs via top-activating tokens and WordNet hierarchies. Use synthetic calibration to derive confidence intervals.
pilots.pair_identification: WordNet hypernym-hyponym + top-100 activating token overlap
pilots.corpus: OpenWebText subset (1k prompts for co-occurrence statistics)
pilots.pass_criteria: At least 5 parent-child pairs identified; metric scores computable for all pairs; confidence intervals from synthetic calibration are non-degenerate (width > 0 and < 1)
pilots.failure_action: If < 5 pairs identified: expand WordNet search or use spelling hierarchies; if metrics fail: use simpler metric variant; if GemmaScope available, try as alternative

round_1_2_lessons_applied: Replaced real-SAE measurement (no ground-truth) with synthetic data where ground-truth is known
Switched from semantic overlap metric to mechanistic ablation-based metric (Chanin et al.)
Eliminated degenerate probe tasks (AUC=1.0) by using synthetic ground-truth labels
Removed dependency on Gemma-2-2B multi-width SAEs (pilot uses GPT-2-small as fallback)
Added confidence intervals derived from synthetic calibration curves
Designed modular pipeline: synthetic validation first, then transfer to real SAEs
